title: Line 22300 - Deduction for PPIP premiums on self-employment income - Canada.ca
content:
## Line 22300 – Deduction for PPIP premiums on self-employment income
If you were a resident of Quebec on December 31 , 2025, you have to pay PPIP premiums if one of the following conditions applies:
- Your net self-employment income on lines 13499 to 14300 of your return is $2,000 or more
- The total of your employment income (including from outside Canada) and your net self-employment income is $2,000 or more
If you were not a resident of Quebec on December 31 , 2025, this line does not apply to you.

## Completing your tax return
Complete Schedule 10 to calculate your PPIP premiums and attach it to your paper return . You can claim 43.736% of your total PPIP premiums on line 22300 of your return.
Claim on line 22300 of your return, in dollars and cents, the amount from line 9 of Schedule 10 . Claim the same amount on your Revenu Québec Income Tax Return , if applicable.
The maximum amount you can claim on line 22300 of your return is $376.32.

## Forms and publications
- Schedule 10, Employment Insurance (EI) and Provincial Parental Insurance Plan (PPIP) Premiums
- Quebec Parental Insurance Plan

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
